Next to go back many days just to make sure that this model holds up for all historical data... `way_earlier_dt=today_dt-dt.timedelta(days=365) dict_way_earlier={'date': way_earlier_dt.strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")} r=requests.get(' print(r.text) {"data":{"amount":"415.23","currency":"USD"},...}` Indeed this returns a reasonable price for that datetime. Subtracting six hours as a final check... By default, returns 350 (max 350). #### Response A successful response. evidences.title: Coinbase API Cheat Sheet for Developers
evidences.content: ## What is the Coinbase API Cheat Sheet? This Coinbase API cheat sheet helps you get easy access to most of the most commonly used commands for the Coinbase API. The Coinbase API is a RESTful service that allows developers to interact with Coinbase’s platform programmatically. You can access account balances, retrieve deposit and withdrawal histories, place orders, monitor crypto prices, and much more. It includes both public and private endpoints, where public endpoints provide access to general market data, and private endpoints require authentication, granting access to user-specific resources, such as wallet details and trade history. [...] ### List Orders: ``` bash GET /api/v3/brokerage/orders/historical ``` ### Place Order: ``` bash POST /api/v3/brokerage/orders ``` ### Request Body Example: ``` json { "client_order_id": "unique_id", "product_id": "BTC-USD", "side": "BUY", "order_configuration": { "market_market_ioc": { "quote_size": "50.00" } } } ``` Orders are more flexible here, and you can choose from market, limit, and stop orders. ## Section 7: Deposit and Withdrawals Although Coinbase doesn’t offer programmatic access to initiate fiat deposits or withdrawals due to...
